title: Androcalva leiperi
taxonomy: Androcalva leiperi is classified within the family Cunoniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the ANZAC woods, although they are native to Australia and New Zealand. The genus Androcalva is part of this family and is native to Australia. The classification of Androcalva leiperi places it within the kingdom Plantae. The specific classification of Androcalva leiperi includes its scientific name, binomial nomenclature, and its ranking within the plant taxonomy.

intro: Androcalva leiperi, also known as Leiper's Androcalva or Leiper's pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Androcalva leiperi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Androcalva leiperi is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are available in health food stores and online.
